description: Prevents silent UI drift between an approved design and the implementation plan.

hooks.prompt:
Check whether this session is at risk of an approved UI design being silently trimmed in the implementation plan.

Evaluation steps:
1. Was a UI design/preview generated this session (e.g. via a frontend-design skill) and approved/accepted by the user? If not, approve.
2. After approval, was an implementation plan written or UI code produced? If not, approve.
3. If both happened: enumerate every concrete visual element in the approved design (sections, copy and description lines, icons, SVG textures, glow/effects, colors, layout/spacing) and check each one is present in the plan or implementation.
4. If any element exists in the approved design but is missing or simplified in the plan/implementation, AND there is no record of the user explicitly approving its omission, set decision to block and write the reason in the user's language, listing each dropped or simplified element, and remind: reopen the approved design and diff it element-by-element, explicitly flag any intended omissions to the user first, and note that unit tests do not verify visual fidelity.
5. If everything is faithfully reproduced, or all omissions were approved, approve.

Only judge UI-fidelity issues; do not block stopping for any other reason.
